purpose: Track how each generated Python command executes implementation-independent command/operation contracts while keeping unavoidable live package behavior explicit.

status_meanings.portable-codegen-primitive-executed: Generated Python command loads a generated operation artifact whose steps are implemented by codegen-owned portable primitives only.
status_meanings.domain-runtime-primitive-via-ir: Generated Python command loads a generated operation artifact and executes it through codegen-owned dataflow, but one or more steps are package-domain runtime primitives rather than portable codegen primitives.
status_meanings.generated-runtime-handler: Generated Python command executes a command-generation rendered runtime handler from declarative metadata without an AW-owned _run_* adapter, while delegated package CLIs retain their own command semantics.
status_meanings.accepted-hand-owned-runtime-primitive: Generated Python parser/dispatch/interface metadata selects an operation id, then a thin _run_*_adapter delegates to hand-owned package runtime primitives. Each accepted entry must declare runtime_boundary_class and runtime_boundary_reason so full completion can distinguish package-specific judgment/live inspection/mutation orchestration from generic deterministic runtime debt.
status_meanings.compatibility-runtime-handler: Temporary unaccepted fallback status; full Python completion must reject this status.
status_meanings.runtime_boundary_class: Classifies accepted hand-owned runtime entries as package-specific-judgment, live-workspace-inspection, mutation-orchestration, front-door-dispatch, provider-integration, or generic-deterministic-runtime-debt. generic-deterministic-runtime-debt blocks full-generated-cli-complete.

hand_owned_runtime_code: workspace config loading and payload assembly
entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Operation dataflow, exact selected-output projection, config JSON/tiny/compact/full/selected output emission through output.emit text_views, and direct generated callable conformance for config.selected-output.success are generated; the package-domain primitive step still owns config loading, local override state, module descriptor validation, and payload assembly.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Replace each package-domain primitive in this operation with schema-backed codegen-owned primitives once the behavior is deterministic file/data/template/schema/output work rather than package policy, live inspection, lifecycle safety, or external-adapter judgment.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Classified as package-specific-judgment; generated operation IR and codegen-owned run_operation_steps own the dataflow, payload.project owns exact selector projection, and output.emit consumes schema-backed config text_views declared in operation IR for JSON/tiny/compact/full/selected text. config.selected-output.success proves selected-output behavior through generated Python and TypeScript operation callables instead of wrapper-smoke CLI execution, and Python generated command conformance proves the regenerated config emitter at the CLI boundary. workspace.config.load remains the explicit package-boundary call for config layering and payload assembly. This entry is not accepted as portable codegen coverage, and full-completion proof rejects it if the boundary class is generic-deterministic-runtime-debt.

entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Local chat checkpoint writes apply package-specific continuity limits, gitignored local path policy, and non-evidence semantics.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent local checkpoint merge/write behavior as schema-backed IR steps once local-only ignored-record policy can be expressed without package-specific runtime judgment.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Classified as package-specific-judgment; generated command surfaces own parser and help projection while workspace runtime primitives own local checkpoint merge/write semantics, local-only limits, and non-evidence policy.

entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Final-response admission applies package-specific terminal outcome custody, continuation operation selection, and local-only checkpoint semantics.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent host final-response custody and continuation dispatch as schema-backed protocol primitives once the host-loop boundary is stable.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Classified as package-specific-judgment; generated command surfaces own parser and help projection while workspace runtime primitives own terminal outcome admission, continuation execution, and local-only resume checkpointing.

entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Autopilot run owns the ordinary host executor loop, final-response custody admission, continuation re-entry, and local-only checkpoint semantics.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent ordinary host executor custody and continuation dispatch as schema-backed protocol primitives once the autopilot boundary is stable.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Classified as package-specific-judgment; generated command surfaces own parser and help projection while workspace runtime primitives own the ordinary executor loop, terminal outcome admission, continuation execution, and local-only resume checkpointing.

entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Carry inspection applies package-specific current-work ownership, lifecycle, and ignored-local non-evidence semantics.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent carry ownership and lifecycle projection as schema-backed IR once current-work binding policy is portable.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Generated command surfaces own parser/help; workspace runtime owns exact local carry ownership and lifecycle policy.

hand_owned_runtime_code: exact carry selection and atomic local index write
entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Carry selection validates exact active ownership and writes ignored-local advisory selection atomically.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent exact carry selection as schema-backed IR once current-work ownership policy is portable.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Generated command surfaces own parser/help; workspace runtime owns exact selection and non-evidence policy.

hand_owned_runtime_code: exact-key and context-revision guarded carry lifecycle repair
entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Carry pruning applies exact selection, optimistic context identity, stale evidence, and cross-context preservation policy.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent guarded local lifecycle repair as schema-backed IR once carry staleness policy is portable.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Generated command surfaces own parser/help; workspace runtime owns exact stale-repair and preservation policy.

entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Local work-thread pruning applies package-specific candidate selection, ignored-local path limits, and non-evidence semantics.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent local prune-candidate deletion as schema-backed IR steps once local-only work-thread safety policy can be expressed without package-specific runtime judgment.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Classified as package-specific-judgment; generated command surfaces own parser and help projection while workspace runtime owns local work-thread prune eligibility, local-only path safety, and non-evidence policy.

entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Local work-thread selection applies package-specific candidate validation, ignored-local index preservation, atomic write semantics, and non-evidence advisory-state policy.

hand_owned_runtime_code: local-only session log pointer and log file writes
entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Command-generation owns argparse parser and dispatch metadata for the session-log command; AW runtime owns local-only ignored session log state, containment checks, and compatibility output.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Represent session log status/note/new-session as schema-backed generated operation steps once dynamic ignored-local log path creation and append policy are declarative primitives.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Generated command surfaces own parser, help, and runtime handler dispatch; AW-owned session_logging functions own local-only path policy, pointer validation, optional note append, and status payload compatibility.

hand_owned_runtime_code: workspace.defaults.load and workspace.defaults.select route through the accepted workspace runtime boundary for shared selector prevalidation, defaults resource loading, section/tiny compatibility, and selected-output projection; tiny-router text consumes declared view metadata from workspace_output_views.json
entries.runtime_boundary_reason: Operation dataflow and output emission stay generated, while defaults payload loading, defaults section selection, selector prevalidation, selected-output projection, and compact-answer compatibility are package-runtime policy behind explicit workspace.defaults.load and workspace.defaults.select primitive refs. Tiny-router text consumes declared view metadata from src/agentic_workspace/contracts/workspace_output_views.json. Product truth is stored in src/agentic_workspace/contracts/workspace_defaults/payload.json and copied into generated/workspace/python/_contracts.
entries.what_would_make_portable_later: Replace each package-domain primitive in this operation with schema-backed codegen-owned primitives once the behavior is deterministic file/data/template/schema/output work rather than package policy, live inspection, lifecycle safety, or external-adapter judgment.
entries.generic_behavior_audit: Generated operation IR and codegen-owned run_operation_steps own defaults.report dataflow and output.emit owns JSON/tiny-router text emission. workspace.defaults.load and workspace.defaults.select intentionally import agentic_workspace.workspace_runtime_primitives through accepted exact source-symbol runtime facade entries so shared selector prevalidation rejects invalid selectors before defaults payload construction. This boundary is not accepted as portable codegen coverage; it is stale when selector prevalidation, defaults resource loading, section/tiny compatibility, and selected-output projection can be expressed as stable declarative dataflow.
